title: Transactional Outbox, Relay, Inbox, Sagas, and Compensation · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A multi-agency incident coordination program. They need quorum data safety, RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry and timeout policy, keyed order, stream-versus-queue decisions, a transactional outbox relay, duplicate-safe inbox effects, and compensated cross-service workflows. Planned artifact: Deliver Replicated, Ordered, Replayable Workflow Platform with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.
Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Replica Majority Safety
steps.instruction:
project case — A project messaging team handles case 6783 in a consumer fleet under a shared dependency outage. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.
